---
title: "Week 15: Portfolio and Going Live"
subtitle: "MC 451 Research Methods in Mass Media"
date: "2026-12-01"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
---

## This Week

This is the last teaching week of the semester. Chapter 21 walks you through the structure of a research portfolio --- what goes in it, how the pieces connect, and how to present your work as a coherent whole. Chapter 22 covers the mechanics of going live: rendering your Quarto Book, pushing to GitHub, and enabling GitHub Pages so your portfolio has a public URL.

**Reading:** Chapter 21 (The Portfolio) and Chapter 22 (Going Live)

---

## What You Need to Do This Week

### 1. Read Chapters 21 and 22

Read both chapters before class. As you read, pay attention to:

- How the six sections of the portfolio map to the research process
- The difference between rendering locally and publishing via GitHub Pages
- How `_quarto.yml` controls the structure of a Quarto Book
- What makes a portfolio look professional versus thrown together

### 2. Complete the CITI Ethics Certification (below)

### 3. Begin Assembling Your Final Portfolio

Download the `mc451-final-project` template via coursepackR or Blackboard. Open the project in RStudio and start placing your writing into the pre-built `.qmd` files. You do not need to finish this week --- but you should have the project open, rendering, and partially populated before you leave class.

---

## Assignment: CITI Ethics Certification (25 pts)

**Due:** Friday 11:59 PM | **Submit to:** Blackboard (PDF upload)

### The Mission

Before any researcher collects data involving human subjects, they must understand the ethical framework governing that work. The CITI Program is the industry-standard training for research ethics. Your certification is valid for three years and appears on your resume.

### Why It Matters for Content Analysis

Content analysis of public media does not typically require IRB review, but it requires ethical awareness. When does a public post become private? What obligations exist when data includes identifiable individuals? How do you handle sensitive content? The CITI training gives you a framework for answering these questions --- not just for this course, but for any research you do in the future.

### Steps

1. **Log in through SIUE SSO** at [citiprogram.org](https://www.citiprogram.org/). Do NOT create a separate account --- use the SIUE single sign-on so your certification is linked to the university.
2. **Find your courses.** Navigate to Institutional Courses > View Courses > Southern Illinois University Edwardsville.
3. **If no courses appear,** click Add a Course and select **Social and Behavioral Research**.
4. **Complete all required modules.** This takes 2--3 hours across 1--2 sittings. You do not need to finish in one session --- CITI saves your progress.
5. **Download your Certificate of Completion** as a PDF when you finish.

### Submission

Upload your certificate to Blackboard as a PDF.

**File name:** `Lastname_CITICert.pdf`

### Grading

This assignment is graded on a completion basis. A valid CITI certificate submitted by the deadline = 25 points. No partial credit.

### Already Certified?

If you completed CITI within the past 3 years at SIUE or another institution, email Dr. Leith before the deadline with your existing certificate attached. Do not re-take the training unnecessarily.

---

## Connecting Forward

Week 16 is a remote work week --- no class, no reading. Use it to build, render, and publish your final portfolio. Week 17 is finals --- the portfolio is due. Everything you do this week and next is in service of that final submission.

The portfolio template gives you the structure. The chapters give you the content. Your job over the next two weeks is to bring them together into a single, polished, publicly accessible research document.

---

## Weekly Journal (10 pts)

Your journal entry for this week is included in this download as `journal_week_15.qmd`. Open it in Obsidian, choose your reflection approach (Connector, Troubleshooter, or Critic), and write 250--300 words.

**Due:** Friday 11:59 PM | **Submit:** Push to GitHub via GitHub Desktop, then submit rendered PDF to Blackboard.

---

## Getting Help

- **Course website:** [Open Methods Hub](https://sim-lab-siue.github.io/liaison-program/) --- check here first
- **Email:** aleith@siue.edu (allow 24 hours Mon--Fri)
- **Office hours:** MW 10:00--11:30 AM and 1:30--3:00 PM, Dunham Hall 1017
- **Discord:** APLeith
